Value: 209,Category: Cy; Value: 209","CreationDate":"2025-12-03","SUBJECT":"AGRICULTURE, WALES","CATEGORY":"AGRICULTURE, WALES,AMAETHYDDIAETH, CYMRU","SUMMARY":"These Regulations amend assimilated law and other legislation in relation to the Basic Payment Scheme (“BPS”) established under Regulation (EU) No1307/2013 (“the Direct Payments Regulation”). These Regulations give the Welsh Ministers the power to taper payments under the BPS from 1 January 2026 until the end of 2028 when the BPS will close. These Regulations are made by the Welsh Ministers under powers conferred by section 16(1) of the Agriculture (Wales) Act 2023.,Mae’r Rheoliadau hyn yn diwygio cyfraith a gymathwyd a deddfwriaeth arall mewn perthynas â Chynllun y Taliad Sylfaenol (“BPS”) a sefydlwyd o dan Reoliad (EU) Rhif 1307/2013 (“y Rheoliad Taliadau Uniongyrchol”). Mae’r Rheoliadau hyn yn rhoi’r pŵer i Weinidogion Cymru i dapro taliadau o dan BPS o 1 Ionawr 2026 tan ddiwedd 2028 pan fydd BPS yn cau. Gwneir y Rheoliadau hyn gan Weinidogion Cymru o dan bwerau a roddir gan adran 16(1) o Ddeddf Amaethyddiaeth (Cymru) 2023.","":""}

This item of legislation is currently only available in its original format. Welsh Statutory Instruments 2025 No. 1282 (W. 209) Agriculture, Wales The Basic Payment Scheme (Tapering, Payment Entitlements and Closure) (Wales) Regulations 2025 Made 3 December 2025 Coming into force in accordance with regulation 1(2) and (3). The Welsh Ministers make the following Regulations in exercise of the powers conferred by section 16(1) of the Agriculture (Wales) Act 2023( 1 ). In accordance with section 50(6) of that Act, a draft of these Regulations has been laid before, and approved by a resolution of, Senedd Cymru. Title, coming into force and application 1. —(1) The title of these Regulations is the Basic Payment Scheme (Tapering, Payment Entitlements and Closure) (Wales) Regulations 2025. (2) These Regulations come into force on 1 January 2026, subject to paragraph (3). (3) Regulation 3(2) of, and the Schedule to, the Regulations come into force on 1 January 2029. (4) These Regulations apply in relation to Wales. Interpretation 2. In these Regulations— “ basic payment scheme ” (“ cynllun y taliad sylfaenol ”) means the Basic Payment Scheme under the Direct Payments Regulation; “the Direct Payments Regulation”( 2 ) (“ y Rheoliad Taliadau Uniongyrchol ”) means Regulation (EU) No1307/2013 of the European Parliament and of the Council establishing rules for direct payments to farmers under support schemes within the framework of the common agricultural policy. Closure of the basic payment scheme 3. —(1) The basic payment scheme will cease to exist at the end of 31 December 2028. (2) The Schedule makes provision revoking legislation governing the basic payment scheme. Tapering of payments under the basic payment scheme 4. —(1) The Direct Payments Regulation is amended as follows. (2) After Article 5A (the total ceiling for Wales), insert— “ Article 5B Tapering of payments under the basic payment scheme in Wales 1. The Welsh Ministers may annually reduce payments under the basic payment scheme by such amounts as they consider appropriate. ”  Transfer and use of entitlements 5. —(1) The Direct Payments Regulation is amended as follows. (2) In Article 34 (transfer of payment entitlements), after paragraph (1) insert— “ (1A) Transfer of payment entitlements, by any method, can only take place if it includes the transfer of an eligible hectare in the same transaction from the same transferor. ”  Young farmer payment 6. —(1) Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) No639/2014 of 11 March 2014 supplementing Regulation (EU) No1307/2013 of the European Parliament and of the Council establishing rules for direct payments to farmers under support schemes within the framework of the common agricultural policy and amending Annex X to that Regulation( 3 ), is amended as follows. (2) In Article 25 (transfer of entitlements)— (a) in paragraph (1), after “at any time of the year” insert “providing that each entitlement is transferred with an eligible hectare” ;  (b) after paragraph (2), insert—  “ (3) For the purpose of this Article, “ eligible hectare ” has the meaning given in Article 32(2) of Regulation (EU) No1307/2013 . ”  (3) In Article 49 (access of legal persons to the payment for young farmers), after paragraph (4) insert— “ (5) The annual payment for young farmers in Wales referred to in Article 50(1) of Regulation (EU) No1307/2013 is only available to those young farmers who are already receiving such payment before 1 January 2026. ”  Closure of the national and regional reserves in the Direct Payments Regulation 7. —(1) The Direct Payments Regulation is amended as follows. (2) Omit Article 30 (establishment and use of the national reserve or regional reserves). (3) Omit Article 31 (replenishment of the national reserve or regional reserves). Closure of the national and regional reserves in Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) No639/2014 8. —(1) Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) No639/2014 is amended as follows. (2) Omit Article 26 (reversion to the national or regional reserve due to retention on transfer of payment entitlements). (3) Omit Article 28 (establishment of payment entitlements from the national or regional reserve under Article 30(6) of Regulation (EU) No1307/2013 ). (4) Omit Article 29 (establishment of payment entitlements from the national or regional reserve under Article 30(7) of Regulation (EU) No1307/2013 ). (5) Omit Article 30 (further rules on the establishment of payment entitlements from the national or regional reserve). Closure of the national and regional reserves in the Common Agricultural Policy Basic Payment and Support Schemes (Wales) Regulations 2015 9. —(1) The Common Agricultural Policy Basic Payment and Support Schemes (Wales) Regulations 2015( 4 ) are amended as follows. (2) Omit regulation 10 (basic payment scheme entitlements). (3) Omit regulation 11 (transfer of entitlements). (4) Omit regulation 18 (national reserve). Additional amendments in the Direct Payments Regulation 10. —(1) The Direct Payments Regulation is amended as follows. (2) In Article 5A (the total ceiling for Wales)— (a) in paragraph (1), for “must” substitute “may” ;
